thesis: Amcor plc is a global packaging manufacturer across flexibles and rigids that completed an all-stock merger with Berry Global on April 30, 2025, creating a ~$23 billion pro forma revenue platform. FY2025 operating cash flow was $1.39 billion; after $0.58 billion of capital spending, owner cash is about $1.75 per share on 462 million shares outstanding.
why_market_wrong: No classic dated mispricing signal. Generalists may still price Amcor as a low-growth commodity packager with merger indigestion, underweighting scale, customer diversification, and synergy potential from Berry. Return here is normalized owner cash at price through integration, not a sum-of-the-parts on goodwill.

key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 OCF $1.39B less capex $0.58B ÷ 462.3M shares (Q3 FY2026 10-Q)
Berry synergies phase in; 9M FY2026 revenue +72% YoY [Assumption in scenarios.base]
Lawrence base scenario exit on owner cash
Component sum after contract backfill (platform + synergy − reserve)

open_questions: Berry synergy run-rate: when does management disclose quantified cost savings versus integration charges?
Deleveraging path: can FCF conversion improve as integration capex normalizes post Q3 FY2026 spike?
Live price confirmation for sizing (human_review.live_price_confirmed still false).
